notes: openwhisk_standalone means the workload is expected to run through SeBS' OpenWhisk backend on a single-host OpenWhisk standalone deployment.
local_standalone means the workload can be run directly by cosmos-bench-profiler standalone --workload command without depending on OpenWhisk activation semantics.

workloads.name: sleep
blockers: low resource signal; useful only as a lifecycle/control workload

workloads.name: uploader
blockers: needs SeBS storage binding and object setup before a faithful local adapter is available

workloads.name: thumbnailer
blockers: needs storage-backed input/output adapter for standalone mode
